title: Agriculture, forestry, and fishing, value added per worker (constant 2015 US$)
definition: Agriculture, forestry, and fishing corresponds to ISIC (Rev. 4) divisions 01-03 and includes the exploitation of vegetal and animal natural resources, comprising the activities of growing of crops, raising and breeding of animals, harvesting of timber and other plants, animals or animal products from a farm or their natural habitats.Value added is the contribution to the economy by a producer or an industry or an institutional sector, which is estimated by the total value of output produced and deducting the total value of intermediate consumption of goods and services used to produce that output. This indicator is expressed in constant prices, meaning the series has been adjusted to account for price changes over time. The reference year for this adjustment is 2015. This indicator is expressed in United States dollars.

editorial.shortHook: A candidate map for agriculture value per worker.
editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include Israel, Canada, Iceland, while the lightest include Zambia, Burundi, Congo, Dem. Rep.; that contrast is the fastest way to read the agriculture value per worker pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Agriculture value per worker may add useful WORLDPRINT variety once source coverage, unit clarity, map interest, and distractor ambiguity are reviewed.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Employment in agriculture can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Male agricultural employment can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Female agricultural employment can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.

editorial.difficultyReason: Expert difficulty: the map can be mistaken for adjacent indicators unless players probe a high, low, and middle country.
regionalSignals: Agriculture maps reward separating productivity, land share, and workforce dependence.
Compare farming belts with arid, forested, and highly urban economies.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Agriculture value per worker is source-valid but more technical or correlated than a default Daily map.
Keep to advanced tiers and review confusion notes before broader promotion.
